trigger: Operator runs 'config acl add table ...' or 'acl-loader update full rules.json'

steps.action: Operator runs 'config acl add table <name> <type>' — config/main.py calls ConfigDBConnector.set_entry('ACL_TABLE', table_name, table_info) writing the new ACL table definition directly to CONFIG_DB. For rule updates, operator runs 'acl-loader update full rules.json' which calls AclLoader.full_update() that writes ACL_RULE entries via ConfigDBConnector.mod_config().

For rule updates: acl-loader update full reads rules from JSON file via load_rules_from_file(), then full_update() deletes all existing ACL_RULE entries and writes new rules to CONFIG_DB ACL_RULE table via ConfigDBConnector.mod_config(). Each rule key is '<table_name>|<rule_name>'.

AclOrch is initialized with TableConnectors for both CONFIG_DB (CFG_ACL_TABLE_TABLE_NAME, CFG_ACL_RULE_TABLE_NAME) and APPL_DB (APP_ACL_TABLE_TABLE_NAME, APP_ACL_RULE_TABLE_NAME) as declared in orchdaemon.cpp. doTask() dispatches CONFIG_DB ACL_TABLE notifications to doAclTableTask(), which parses table type/ports/stage and calls addAclTable(). addAclTable() calls createBindAclTable() which calls AclTable::create() — this invokes sai_acl_api->create_acl_table() and writes the resulting SAI OID to ASIC_DB.

doTask() dispatches CONFIG_DB ACL_RULE notifications to doAclRuleTask(). For each SET entry, AclRule::makeShared() constructs the rule object, then addAclRule() calls AclRule::create() which first calls createCounter() (sai_acl_api->create_acl_counter()) and then createRule() (sai_acl_api->create_acl_entry()). Both SAI OIDs are written to ASIC_DB.

AclRule::create() calls createCounter() which invokes sai_acl_api->create_acl_counter() and writes the counter OID to ASIC_DB. The counter tracks packet and byte hit counts per rule and is linked to the ACL entry via SAI_ACL_ENTRY_ATTR_ACTION_COUNTER.

syncd reads ASIC_DB notifications. processSingleEvent() dispatches CREATE ops to processQuadEvent(SAI_COMMON_API_CREATE). For OID-based objects (ACL_TABLE, ACL_ENTRY, ACL_COUNTER), processQuadEvent() calls processOid() which calls processOidCreate(). processOidCreate() translates VID to RID, then calls m_vendorSai->create(objectType, &objectRid, switchRid, attr_count, attr_list) which invokes the vendor SAI library to program the TCAM/CAM hardware.
steps.output: ACL table, entry, and counter programmed in hardware TCAM/CAM
steps.verify: aclshow -a && show acl table && show acl rule

failure_injection_points.failure: Invalid ACL table type or unsupported port binding
failure_injection_points.symptom: config acl add table fails with error; ACL_TABLE key absent from CONFIG_DB
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check config acl output for ValueError; verify port names exist: show interfaces status; verify ACL type is supported: L2, L3, L3V6, MIRROR, CTRLPLANE

failure_injection_points.failure: acl-loader fails to write rules — JSON parse error or invalid action value
failure_injection_points.symptom: ACL_RULE entries missing from CONFIG_DB after acl-loader run; acl-loader exits with non-zero status
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Run acl-loader with --verbose; check /var/log/syslog for acl-loader errors; validate rules JSON schema manually; check redis-cli -n 4 KEYS 'ACL_RULE|*'

failure_injection_points.failure: orchagent not processing ACL_TABLE — ports not yet ready or table type unsupported
failure_injection_points.symptom: ACL_TABLE present in CONFIG_DB but no ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_ACL_TABLE entry in ASIC_DB; orchagent log shows 'wait for ports ready'
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check orchagent status: docker exec swss supervisorctl status orchagent; check /var/log/swss/orchagent.log for 'AclOrch' errors; verify all bound ports are operationally up: show interfaces status; check CRM ACL table capacity: crm show resources acl-table

failure_injection_points.failure: ACL rule creation deferred — ACL table OID not yet resolved or next-hop missing for redirect action
failure_injection_points.symptom: ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_ACL_TABLE exists but no ACL_ENTRY; orchagent log shows 'Wait for ACL table ... to be created'
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check if ACL table OID is present: redis-cli -n 1 KEYS 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_ACL_TABLE*'; for redirect rules, verify next-hop is resolved in NEIGH_TABLE; check /var/log/swss/orchagent.log for rule-level errors

failure_injection_points.failure: TCAM exhaustion — hardware ACL table is full
failure_injection_points.symptom: sai_acl_api->create_acl_entry returns SAI_STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES; syncd log shows SAI_STATUS_TABLE_FULL; ACL entries stop programming after hitting hardware limit
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check CRM counters: crm show resources acl-entry; check crm show resources acl-counter; check /var/log/syncd.log for SAI_STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES; consider splitting ACL tables across pipeline stages or reducing rule count

failure_injection_points.failure: syncd SAI call failing — vendor SAI returns unexpected error
failure_injection_points.symptom: ACL entries in ASIC_DB but syncd throws SAI_STATUS_FAILURE; traffic not matching expected ACL rules
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check /var/log/syncd.log for SAI error codes; verify ACL match fields are supported by this platform: crm show resources; check syncd status: docker exec syncd supervisorctl status syncd; inspect ASIC vendor logs if available
